elements.title: some buildings in Korean Buddhist temples
elements.contents: ... that some buildings in Korean Buddhist temples (example pictured) also enshrine an indigenous mountain god?

elements.title: an accused Peruvian serial killer
elements.contents: ... that an accused Peruvian serial killer was killed by a psychologist during an interrogation?

elements.title: Unlimited Intimacy
elements.contents: ... that queer-theory professor Tim Dean's book Unlimited Intimacy investigates the sexual subculture of barebacking among gay men?

elements.title: Le brothers
elements.contents: ... that the actor-choreographer Le brothers taught themselves martial arts through YouTube videos and kung fu films?

elements.title: Josephine M. Hagerty House
elements.contents: ... that the Josephine M. Hagerty House was likened to a packing crate and a jail when completed?

elements.title: Lindsay Knapp
elements.contents: ... that future NFL player Lindsay Knapp, while he was in high school, appeared in a commercial playing basketball against Michael Jordan?

elements.title: Popular Socialist Party
elements.contents: ... that Haiti's Popular Socialist Party supported a conservative in the indirect presidential election of 1946 because it believed its candidate would not be popular enough to win?

elements.title: Olivia Book
elements.contents: ... that Olivia Book is a professional ballerina with a congenital limb difference, a rarity in a field that traditionally holds to rigid body standards?

elements.title: Pine Gap Women's Peace Camp
elements.contents: ... that the 1983 Pine Gap Women's Peace Camp in Alice Springs led to many women moving there and making it a regional lesbian capital of Australia?
